_comment_template: TEMPLATE FILE — not a real retrospective entry. Demonstrates the format a 'retrospective' entry takes when written N generations / months / years after the original 'run' entry, comparing the original recommendation to what actually happened. Replace every `<placeholder>` value with real content. The entry must validate against consortium/audit/blind_spot_log.schema.json before being appended to consortium/audit/blind_spot_log.jsonl.
_comment_when_to_write: Write a retrospective entry when: (a) enough time has passed that the recommendation in the referenced run entry has been tested by reality; (b) the outcome is observable; (c) the consortium can honestly compare what was recommended to what happened. There is no fixed retrospect horizon — for some decisions it's 6 months, for others 7 generations. The original run entry's `time_critical_actions` list is one signal of when retrospect is feasible.
_comment_who_writes: Whoever has access to both the original run entry and the actual outcome. This may be a different consenter, a different consortium membership, or a future-self of the original consenter. The `consenter` field in this entry should name the retrospect-writer; the original run entry's consenter remains attached to the run entry.
problem_id: <same problem_id as the run entry being retrospected>

references.original_entry_id: <entry_id of the run entry this retrospects>

frames_consulted: <re-list the frames from the original run, OR the frames the retrospect-writer consulted to evaluate the outcome — be explicit about which>

<frame_id>: <coupling that, in retrospect, this frame missed and that turned out to matter>

productive_disagreements.interpretation: <which frame turned out to be closer to what happened, OR 'both partial' if the disagreement turned out to be a real geometry of the problem>

coating_probes_run.probe: <the probe name from the original run, OR 'retrospective_outcome_check' as a synthetic probe-equivalent>
coating_probes_run.result: <passed | failed | inconclusive — based on whether the original reading matches what actually happened>
coating_probes_run.notes: <what the actual outcome showed vs what the original reading predicted>

recommended_action: <copy the recommended_action from the original run entry verbatim, so this retrospect's reader can compare to actual_outcome>
actual_outcome: <honest description of what happened. Must include: which actions were taken; which were not; what the system did in response; how much of the original recommendation panned out; what the original recommendation missed.>
notes: <retrospect-writer's reflection. Useful content includes: which factors were `hidden_at_decision_time` in the original `seven_generation_tracer` run that turned out to materialize; which frames were systematically over- or under-confident; whether any frame consistently named the issue that turned out to matter and was overruled. This text is the input for the next `calibration_update` entry.>
